title: Admin back-office
depends: accounts · game · golf-data · leagues · scoring · web-shell

rows.value: The operator surface: a sidebar-navigated `/admin` area for correcting tournaments, results, users, golfers, leagues, and picks — plus manual triggers for every sync and rollup the cron normally runs.
Only users whose Better Auth `role` is `admin`. Everyone else is redirected to `/dashboard` by the layout, and rejected with `FORBIDDEN` by the API.

bigIdea.label: Big idea
bigIdea.line: Every admin capability is a tRPC procedure behind `adminProcedure` — the `/admin` layout redirect is UX, the middleware is the boundary.
bigIdea.sub: `src/app/admin/layout.tsx` redirects non-admins, but the real gate is the `adminProcedure` middleware in `src/server/trpc.ts` (web-shell), which throws `FORBIDDEN` unless the session role is `admin`. Destructive overrides carry service-layer guards — last-admin protection, commissioner reassignment, the GLOBAL league is undeletable, exclusion blocks on locked picks — and pick overrides are written to the commissioner audit log and trigger earnings recalculation.

blocks.title: Components
blocks.intro: Six operator consoles (server pages + client mutation forms) over one three-layer API, plus a public health router.

cards.name: Admin gate & layout
cards.purpose: The `/admin` shell: a server layout that checks `trpc.auth.me()` and redirects non-admins, a sidebar nav over all consoles, and an overview dashboard of platform stats.

cards.name: Tournament ops
cards.purpose: List, edit, and exclude tournaments; hand-correct per-golfer results; fire field/results/live-standings syncs and the earnings rollup for one event.

cards.name: User ops
cards.purpose: Search users, flip roles, delete accounts, send password resets and test pick-reminder emails, and yank a member's picks or league memberships.

cards.name: League & golfer ops
cards.purpose: Override any league's settings, membership, and commissioner; delete non-global leagues; correct golfer name/country/world-ranking data.

cards.name: Cron console
cards.purpose: The last 20 `CronLog` runs with their JSON payloads, plus a button that runs the same `runFullSync` the scheduled `/api/cron/sync` route runs.

cards.name: Admin API
cards.purpose: The `admin.*` tRPC surface — 35 `adminProcedure` procedures, a service layer holding every override guard, and a repository of cross-table Kysely read models and cascade deletes.

cards.name: Health probes
cards.purpose: Three public liveness checks — process, database, and the ESPN upstream — for uptime monitoring.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: Every console page talks tRPC (server pages via `serverTRPC`, client forms via `useTRPC` mutations). All `admin.*` calls pass the `adminProcedure` gate before the service fans out — to its own repository for reads and overrides, and to neighbor services for syncs, rollups, and password resets.

diagram.d2:
direction: right
operator: { class: actor }
"/admin pages"
"adminProcedure gate": { class: changed }
"admin service"
"admin repository"
Postgres: { class: store }
"golf-data sync"
"earnings-core"
"Better Auth"
operator -> "/admin pages"
"/admin pages" -> "adminProcedure gate": "tRPC admin.*"
"adminProcedure gate" -> "admin service"
"admin service" -> "admin repository"
"admin repository" -> Postgres
"admin service" -> "golf-data sync": manual syncs
"admin service" -> "earnings-core": rollup
"admin service" -> "Better Auth": password reset
diagram.caption: `health.*` sits outside this path — its three probes are `publicProcedure` and query the db / ESPN directly.

components.name: Admin gate & layout
detail: `layout.tsx` is a server component that calls `trpc.auth.me()` and does `if (!me || me.role !== "admin") redirect("/dashboard")` before rendering anything. It wraps every `/admin` route in a sidebar nav: Overview, Tournaments, Users, Golfers, Leagues, Promo Codes, Cron Logs. The Promo Codes link points at `/admin/promos`, whose pages live in the [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html) domain — the nav spans both.

`page.tsx` is the overview dashboard: one `admin.stats` call renders total users, leagues, picks this season, the currently-live tournament (linking into tournament ops), and the last cron run's success flag and duration — a one-glance health check before digging into any console.

files.name: app/admin/layout.tsx
files.desc: Role-gated server layout + sidebar nav over all admin consoles

files.name: app/admin/page.tsx
files.desc: Overview dashboard: platform counts, live tournament, last cron run

connections.body: `trpc.auth.me()` from the [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html) auth router to read the session role.
`admin.stats` — counts assembled by the [Admin API](#c-api) repository plus the latest `CronLog` row.
`serverTRPC` from [web-shell](../domain-web-shell/domain-web-shell.html) — all server pages in this domain fetch through it.

components.name: Tournament ops
detail: The list page filters by season, status, and name, with an `EXCLUDED` pseudo-status that maps to the `excluded` boolean filter, and derives a `LIVE` badge for `UPCOMING` tournaments whose start date has passed. The detail page loads via `admin.tournament` and hands off to the `TournamentAdmin` client component.

`TournamentAdmin` is the busiest console: an edit form (name, course, dates, purse, estimated purse, status) with a pair of timezone helpers — `toInputDateTime` / `fromInputDateTime` — that treat every `datetime-local` value as US Eastern regardless of the operator's browser timezone; an exclude toggle that surfaces the service's `PRECONDITION_FAILED` (locked picks exist) as a **Force Exclude** escalation button; and four sync buttons — Sync Field, Sync Results, Sync Live Standings, and Run Earnings Rollup (hardcoded to `GLOBAL_LEAGUE_ID`).

`TournamentResults` renders the per-golfer results table with inline row editing of finish position, earnings, score-to-par, and rounds — typing `MC` (missed cut) stores position `0`. This is the hand-correction path when ESPN's payout data is wrong, and its edits feed directly into what [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) rolls up.

files.name: app/admin/tournaments/page.tsx
files.desc: Season/status/search list with EXCLUDED filter and LIVE derivation

files.name: app/admin/tournaments/[id]/page.tsx
files.desc: Server loader — `admin.tournament`, `notFound()` on miss

files.name: app/admin/tournaments/[id]/tournament-admin.tsx
files.desc: Edit form, ET datetime conversion, exclusion + force flow, sync controls, field table

files.name: app/admin/tournaments/[id]/tournament-results.tsx
files.desc: Inline-editable results table (MC → position 0)

exports.name: TournamentAdmin
exports.desc: Client console for one tournament: edit, exclude, sync

exports.name: TournamentResults
exports.desc: Inline results editor, invalidates its own query on save

connections.body: Corrected `tournamentResult` rows — the raw input for every leaderboard and season standings number.

components.name: User ops
detail: The list page searches name/email/username and shows each user's league count and picks this season (two aggregate queries stitched in the repository). The detail page loads the user plus their leagues and season picks, along with upcoming tournaments to power the test-reminder dropdown.

`UserDetail` is the account-surgery console: toggle role between `user` and `admin` (the service blocks self-demotion and demoting/deleting the last admin), send a password reset (routed through Better Auth's `requestPasswordReset`), send a test pick-reminder email for a chosen upcoming tournament, remove the user from any league, remove any individual pick (via the audited `admin.adminRemovePick`), and a confirm-gated delete that cascades picks, memberships, season rows, and auth records — refused while the user still commissions any league.

files.name: app/admin/users/page.tsx
files.desc: Searchable user list with league/pick counts and role badges

files.name: app/admin/users/[id]/page.tsx
files.desc: Server loader — user detail + upcoming tournaments for the test reminder

files.name: app/admin/users/[id]/user-detail.tsx
files.desc: Role, password reset, test reminder, membership/pick removal, delete

exports.name: UserDetail
exports.desc: Client console wiring six admin mutations for one account

connections.body: `admin.adminRemovePick` — deletes via [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html)'s pick repository and audit-logs via [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html)' `createCommissionerAction`.

components.name: League & golfer ops
detail: League ops: the list filters by type (GLOBAL/PRIVATE), payment status, and name. `LeagueAdmin` edits any league's settings — name, `maxGolferUses`, `burnStalePicks`, `showPicksBeforeLock`, `requireApproval` — and reassigns the commissioner from a dropdown of current members. It adds members through a live `admin.users` search (existing members filtered out), removes any member except the commissioner, and offers a Danger Zone delete that is simply not rendered for the GLOBAL league (the service refuses it server-side too).

Golfer ops is the small sibling under `src/app/admin/golfers`: a searchable list showing world ranking and events played, and `GolferAdmin`, a three-field form correcting name, country, and world ranking — the fix-up path when ESPN data comes in wrong or incomplete.

files.name: app/admin/leagues/page.tsx
files.desc: Type/payment/search league list with member counts

files.name: app/admin/leagues/[id]/page.tsx
files.desc: Server loader — `admin.league` with members

files.name: app/admin/leagues/[id]/league-admin.tsx
files.desc: Settings, commissioner reassignment, membership add/remove, delete

files.name: app/admin/golfers/page.tsx

files.name: app/admin/golfers/[id]/page.tsx
files.desc: Server loader — `admin.golfer`

files.name: app/admin/golfers/[id]/golfer-admin.tsx
files.desc: Name/country/world-ranking correction form

exports.desc: Client console for one league: settings + membership + delete

connections.body: League settings mirror what [leagues](../domain-leagues/domain-leagues.html) commissioners self-serve — this console is the same knobs without the commissioner-only restriction.

components.name: Cron console
detail: `page.tsx` renders the last 20 cron runs from `admin.cronLogs`: success/error badge, US-Eastern timestamp, duration, and the stored JSON response pretty-printed per run — the primary debugging view when a nightly sync misbehaves.

`RunCronButton` fires `admin.runFullSync`, which the admin service re-exports verbatim from golf-data's `cron-sync` service — so the button runs *exactly* the code path the scheduled `/api/cron/sync` route runs, and its result lands in the same `CronLog` table the page displays.

files.name: app/admin/cron/page.tsx
files.desc: Last 20 CronLog entries with parsed JSON payloads

files.name: app/admin/cron/run-cron-button.tsx
files.desc: Manual `admin.runFullSync` trigger with inline status

exports.name: RunCronButton
exports.desc: Client button — same code path as the scheduled cron

components.name: Admin API
detail: `routers/admin.ts` mounts 35 procedures on `adminProcedure`, spanning stats, tournaments (+results, field, exclusion, syncs), users, golfers, leagues, picks, promo codes, and cron. The router stays thin — zod input, service call — with one exception: `testPickReminder` calls accounts' `sendTestPickReminder` service directly instead of going through `services/admin.ts`.

`services/admin.ts` is where the override guards live: existence checks that turn into `NOT_FOUND`; `updateUserRole`/`deleteUser` refuse self-demotion, self-deletion, and touching the last admin (`countAdmins`); `deleteUser` also refuses while the user commissions any league (`findLeaguesByCommissioner`); `removeUserFromLeague` refuses to remove a commissioner; `deleteLeague` refuses the GLOBAL league; `toggleTournamentExclusion` throws `PRECONDITION_FAILED` when locked picks exist unless `force` is passed. `adminUpdatePick`/`adminRemovePick` wrap the pick change and a `commissionerAction` audit row (`ADMIN_UPDATE_PICK` / `ADMIN_REMOVE_PICK`) in `withTransaction`, then recalculate the member's season if the tournament is `COMPLETED`/`FINALIZING`.

`repositories/admin.ts` holds the read models — search lists with escaped `ilike`, aggregate counts (league members, picks this season, events played), and the platform stats query — plus the destructive writes. Its `deleteUser`, `removeUserFromLeague`, and `deleteLeague` run multi-table cascades (picks → season rows → audit rows → memberships → auth rows) inside `db.transaction()` directly, a deviation from the repo convention of accepting an `executor` param and letting services own transactions.

files.name: server/routers/admin.ts
files.desc: 35 `adminProcedure` procedures — the whole operator surface

files.name: server/services/admin.ts
files.desc: Override guards, audited pick mutations, sync/rollup delegation

files.name: server/repositories/admin.ts
files.desc: Cross-table Kysely read models + cascade deletes

exports.name: adminUpdatePick
exports.desc: Service: swap a pick's golfer, audit it, recalc earnings if finalized

exports.name: findUsers
exports.desc: Repo: searchable user list with league + pick aggregates

connections.body: [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html): `recalculateSeasonForLeague` / `recalculateSeasonForMember` from `earnings-core.ts` after result edits and pick overrides.

[payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html)' `/admin/promos` pages — the promo-code procedures (`admin.promoCodes`, `admin.createPromoCode`, `admin.updatePromoCode`) live in this router even though the promo UI and model belong to payments.

components.name: Health probes
detail: `healthRouter` exposes three `publicProcedure` queries: `ping` (process up), `dbCheck` (`SELECT NOW()` through Kysely), and `espnCheck` (fetches the current season's schedule via `espnProvider.getSchedule` and reports event count + first/last event names — a real exercise of the ESPN dependency, not a stub).

It's the one router in the codebase that imports `db` and queries it directly, skipping the service/repository layering — a deliberate pragmatism for probes that must not depend on the layers they're checking. It's also the only public surface in this domain: everything else requires the admin role.

files.name: server/routers/health.ts
files.desc: `ping` / `dbCheck` / `espnCheck` public probes

connections.body: `espnProvider` from [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s ESPN client for the upstream check.
`db` from [web-shell](../domain-web-shell/domain-web-shell.html)'s `lib/db.ts` — directly, bypassing the repository layer by design.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: Honestly: none. Admin owns no Prisma models — it is an operator lens over everyone else's tables.

rows.name: (no owned models)
rows.desc: `tournament`/`golfer`/`cronLog` belong to golf-data, `user` to accounts, `league`/`leagueMember`/`commissionerAction` to leagues, `pick` to game, `promoCode` to payments.

blocks.note: What makes this domain unusual is write reach, not ownership: `repositories/admin.ts` is the one repository that updates and cascade-deletes across nearly every table in the schema.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: The full `admin.*` surface is 35 procedures, every one behind `adminProcedure`; the representative ones below show its span. `health.*` is the domain's only public surface.

exposes.note: Overview counts + live tournament + last cron run
Same code path as the scheduled cron
Guards: no self-demotion, never the last admin
Audited golfer swap + conditional earnings recalc

exposes.desc: public health probes — `health.ping`, `health.dbCheck`, and `health.espnCheck` (the only public procedures in the domain; dbCheck queries `db` directly, a sanctioned layering exception)

blocks.note: One inbound seam worth knowing: [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html)' promo admin pages depend on this router (`admin.promoCodes` / `admin.promoCode` / `admin.createPromoCode` / `admin.updatePromoCode`) — the dependency arrow points *into* admin, not out.
